extra.message: User input from the 'name' parameter is embedded directly into an HTML f-string (line 38) that is then passed to Jinja2's Template().render() (line 41). This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) attacks. An attacker can inject Jinja2 template syntax like {{7*7}} or {{config}} to execute arbitrary code or access sensitive data.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code
CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code

extra.message: Hardcoded secret value 'John Ripper' for SUPER_SECRET_NAME. This sensitive credential is hardcoded in the source code instead of being loaded from environment variables. This exposes the credential to anyone with access to the repository.

Hardcoded secret value '5u93R53Cr3tT0k3n' for SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN. This sensitive credential is hardcoded in the source code instead of being loaded from environment variables. This exposes the token to anyone with access to the repository.

The public_ip value is obtained from an external API response (requests.get at line 31) without validation or sanitization. When this untrusted data is embedded into the HTML template (line 38) and rendered with Jinja2 (line 41), it can lead to SSTI if the external service is compromised or returns malicious content.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code
